title: Mass. House to vote on youth social media ban, strict school cellphone ban
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Massachusetts House to vote on banning social media for children under 14 and requiring parental consent for ages 14-15.
Proposed legislation would enforce a 'bell-to-bell' cellphone ban in all Massachusetts public schools during the school day.
Quincy School Committee nears approval of strict school cellphone ban using Yondr pouches for middle and high school students.

title: Maine father dies after saving his children from a rip current
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Ryan Jennings, 46, died saving his children from a rip current at Juno Beach, Florida on April 1.
Jennings' wife, Emily, credited him for ensuring their children, aged 12 and 9, survived the incident.
A GoFundMe for Jennings' family raised over $170,000 following his heroic actions and tragic death.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon to support neglected and at-risk kids
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Sean Meehan, 28, from Natick, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for The Home For Little Wanderers.
The Home For Little Wanderers supports at-risk children with services like foster care and special education.
Meehan was inspired by his sister's 2023 race and the Boston Marathon's community spirit and resilience.

title: The Boston Marathon didn’t make this list of best marathons in America. Do you think it should have?
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: USA Today omitted the Boston Marathon from its 2026 10Best Readers’ Choice Awards for best marathons in the U.S.
The Boston Marathon, scheduled for April 20, is one of the Abbott World Marathon Majors and draws over 30,000 runners.
Top marathons listed by USA Today include Detroit Free Press Marathon and Cincinnati Flying Pig Marathon.

title: Everything you need to know about camping at Massachusetts state parks this season
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Massachusetts camping season runs from April to November, with over 30 DCR parks offering various accommodations.
Camping accommodations include tent sites, R.V. spots, cabins, and yurts, with new yurts at Clarksburg and Nickerson State Parks.
In-state camping prices range from $17 to $90, while out-of-state prices range from $54 to $230 depending on location.

title: Roman Anthony: Red Sox 2-7 start is ‘unacceptable to the fans’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Red Sox have started the 2026 season with a 2-7 record, the worst in Major League Baseball.
Red Sox outfielder Roman Anthony called the team's early performance 'unacceptable' to fans and their standards.
Boston ranks low offensively and defensively, with poor batting stats and nine errors tied for third-most in the league.

title: Is this really Boston’s most annoying escalator?
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: A Reddit post criticizes a frequently broken escalator at Copley Place, sparking a debate on Boston's most annoying escalators.
Commenters suggest other escalators, including those at Porter Square and South Station, as more frustrating than Copley Place's.
Readers are invited to share their thoughts on Boston's most annoying escalators via e-mail or an online form.

title: NFL analyst predicts two possible Patriots draft trades
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The Patriots hold the 31st pick in the 2026 NFL Draft and have 11 overall selections.
NFL analyst Bill Barnwell predicts a trade with the Browns for the 24th pick, giving up the 31st, 95th, and 191st picks.
Another scenario involves trading with the Jets for the 33rd and 44th picks, exchanging the 31st, 63rd, and 131st picks.

title: Painter claiming injury at Bill Belichick’s house sues him over alleged unsafe work site
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Painter Andrew Jackson sues Bill Belichick for $285,436 over alleged unsafe work conditions at Nantucket property.
Jackson claims he fell and injured his ankle due to unsafe conditions involving plastic sheeting at 45 Fair St.
Belichick purchased the Nantucket home, where the incident allegedly occurred, for $4.8 million in 2024.

title: Woman shot and killed by police in N.H.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A woman was shot and killed by police during a domestic disturbance response in Northfield, New Hampshire, on Monday.
The New Hampshire Attorney General stated that neither the woman's nor the officer's identity has been disclosed.
This incident marks the third use of deadly force by New England police since Saturday, involving earlier shootings in Boston and New Hampshire.
